topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The ballroom air hummed with the clinking of champagne flutes and the swell of a string quartet. I watched the happy couple sway across the polished floor, a familiar, tight knot tightening in my chest. Then, across the crowded reception, I saw him. He was laughing, a sound I hadn't heard in years, and the way the light caught the silver in his hair was exactly the same. My breath hitched, a shallow, almost painful intake of air. I gripped my glass so hard my knuckles turned white. Every nerve ending seemed to vibrate with a sudden, overwhelming rush of things left unsaid, memories flooding back like an unexpected tide. I forced a smile, a brittle thing that felt alien on my face, and steered myself toward the edge of the room, needing space to just… observe the wreckage of what used to be.

[story 2]

It was impossible to look away. I was standing near the buffet, pretending to examine a canapé, but my gaze kept snagging on him. He was talking animatedly with an old colleague, his gestures fluid and easy, and a warmth spread through my core that made my stomach flip. When his eyes flicked my way, for just a second, the world narrowed down to that single point of contact. A strange, soft ache settled behind my ribs. I felt a sudden urge to walk over, to bridge the distance that had grown so vast between us, but my feet remained rooted to the spot. I took a slow, deliberate sip of water, trying to steady the tremor running through my hands. It was a quiet acknowledgment, a deep, internal recognition of a chapter closed, and the bittersweet echo of its final pages.

[story 3]

The music shifted, and for a brief moment, the noise seemed to recede, leaving only the sound of my own pulse drumming against my eardrums. I spotted him near the entrance, a familiar silhouette against the glittering backdrop of the celebration. My practiced composure dissolved instantly. I felt a strange heaviness in my limbs, a desire to just stand there, suspended in time, watching him navigate the crowd. When he turned and caught my eye, a profound stillness washed over me. There was a flicker in his expression—a recognition, perhaps a shadow of something shared—and in that instant, all the careful walls I had built around my feelings crumbled. I managed a small nod, a gesture heavy with unspoken history, before quickly turning away, needing to retreat before the tide of feeling pulled me under completely.
